kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: The artisan in Oldwall's levy is higher than both Aldric and the Tanner.
Cooper, Mason, and Weaver go with Aldric, Cedric, and 13 coins, in some order.
At least one of the Blacksmith, the Mason, the artisan with 9 coins, and the artisan with 11 coins belongs to the Market Ward.
Exactly one of Cedric and Wulfric belongs to the Hill Ward.
Wulfric goes with exactly two of Cooper, Highrow, and 11 coins.
Exactly one of (the members of the North Side, Wulfric, and the artisan with 11 coins) goes with (Tanner or Cooper).
Both Cedric does not go with 9 coins and 10 coins goes with Blacksmith if and only if 13 coins goes with Kingsford.
Between Matilda and 10 coins, one goes with Blacksmith and the other with Tanner.
More members of the Hill Ward are in the Joiners' Guild than members of the River Ward.
10 coins goes with exactly two of Rowan, Blacksmith, and Highrow.
Either Cedric belongs to the South Side or both members of the Joiners' Guild belong to the South Side (but not both).
No members of the Ironmongers' Guild are in the South Side.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
